output:
A typical person would answer **Yes**.

This is a version of the **Knauer/Side-Effect Effect** (related to the "Chairman" cases by Joshua Knobe). Research consistently shows that when an agent performs an action knowing it will cause a harmful side effect — even if that harm isn't their goal — most people judge the harmful outcome as **intentional**.

The key factors driving this intuition:

- The hunter **knew** the bullet would hit the bird-watcher.
- He **chose to shoot anyway**.
- The side effect was **harmful**.

People tend to say a foreseen bad side effect was brought about intentionally, even though the agent's *goal* was only to kill the deer. (Interestingly, if the side effect were positive, most people would say it was *not* intentional — that asymmetry is the core of the Knobe Effect.)
